opinion.h1: DAHL v. REPUBLICAN STATE COMMITTEE, 393 U.S. 408 (1969)
opinion.h4: DAHL ET AL. v. REPUBLICAN STATE COMMITTEE ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT FOR THE WESTERN DISTRICT OF WASHINGTON. No. 596. Decided January 20, 1969.
opinion.p: The judgment of the District Court is vacated and the cause is remanded in order that the District Court may enter a fresh decree from which appellants may, if they wish, perfect a timely appeal to the Court of Appeals. Moody v. Flowers, 387 U.S. 97.
MR. JUSTICE HARLAN, believing that jurisdiction lies in this Court, would affirm the judgment below.
